response.justification: The candidate is a strong and versatile senior engineer, but his profile is misaligned with this role's core requirements. His career centers on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), with only a brief 7-month stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments over a decade ago. The role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise, message-broker architectures, MQTT/IoT interfaces, and IEC 62443 secure development practices in industrial automation — none of which are evidenced. His DLang low-latency systems work shows transferable systems-programming aptitude, but not enough to offset the gap in required C++ depth and industrial cybersecurity experience.
strengths: Systems-level experience with low-latency, high-throughput services (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and mentoring/teaching track record
Broad polyglot background spanning embedded, desktop, web, and mobile
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience — a single short engagement ending in 2015
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, or OT protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP3)
No message-broker or Azure cloud experience cited; recent work is web/product focused
